summary: Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end, signaling a major escalation in drone warfare. Ukraine conducted a prisoner exchange returning 160 soldiers home and struck Russian military vessels in occupied Crimea, including the 'Volga,' 'Vyakta,' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk.' Multiple Ukrainian regions faced aerial threats with drone attacks on Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and Poltava oblasts, while a MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airfield.
key_points: Russia plans to escalate FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by the end of the year, according to Commander-in-Chief Syrsky. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193)
Ukraine struck Russian military vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyakta' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, targeting systems for underwater reconnaissance and mine-laying. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)
A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian soldiers home, all captured in 2022, including defenders of Mariupol, Azovstal, and multiple front-line directions. (Source: @UkraineNow/82195)
Ukrainian drones attacked the Russian 'Azot' chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, one of Russia's largest chemical facilities, damaging power lines and a residential building. (Source: @UkraineNow/82184)
A MiG-31K capable of carrying the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal ballistic missile took off from Savastleyka airfield, posing a significant threat to multiple regions. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082)

Multiple drone groups targeted Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Chernihiv, and Poltava oblasts, with Russian tactical aviation launching guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia injuring at least six. (Source: @kpszsu/66530, @UkraineNow/82190)

Three Polish politicians declined Ukrainian awards in solidarity with Volyn massacre victims, as Ukraine-Poland relations deteriorate amid Budanov calling the scandal a 'terrible mistake.' (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31192, @ukr_leaks_eng/31182)
European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainians to March 2028, excluding men aged 23-60 and those already granted protection. (Source: @UkraineNow/82187)

sections.executive_summary: Russia plans to escalate FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end, signaling a major shift in front-line drone warfare intensity. Ukraine struck Russian military vessels in occupied Crimea and conducted a 160-person prisoner exchange, while drone attacks hit both sides' infrastructure and civilian areas. A MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka, and Russian tactical aviation bombed Zaporizhzhia, indicating heightened operational tempo across multiple fronts.

points: Ukrainian forces struck Russian military vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyakta' along with ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea. These vessels were designed to deploy underwater reconnaissance systems and lay mines targeting ships, pipelines, and cables. Ukrainian forces also struck weaponry and a radar station from an S-400 complex covering the Kerch Strait area. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian soldiers home, all captured in 2022. The freed personnel included members of the Armed Forces, State Special Transport Service, National Guard, and Border Guards who defended Mariupol, Azovstal, and positions across Donetsk, Luhansk, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Sumy directions. (Source: @UkraineNow/82195)

Ukrainian drones attacked the Russian 'Azot' chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, described as one of Russia's largest chemical facilities. The attack also damaged a power line and a private residential building, with a woman reportedly injured and receiving medical assistance. (Source: @UkraineNow/82184)

An FPV drone attack on Nikopol killed 2 people and injured 12, including two 12-year-old girls who were hospitalized in moderate condition. A minibus was damaged in the attack. (Source: @UkraineNow/82209)

Commander-in-Chief Syrsky stated Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end, indicating Ukraine must prepare for a fivefold increase in drone threats along the front lines. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193)

Foreign Minister Sybiga stated Belarus is not expected to enter the war, suggesting Ukraine will mirror any Russian attempt to draw Belarus into the conflict. Budanov called the Poland scandal a 'terrible mistake' but acknowledged a 'contradiction' rather than a conflict. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191, @ukr_leaks_eng/31182)

Three Polish politicians including former Defense Minister Blaszczak declined Ukrainian awards in solidarity with Volyn massacre victims. The European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainians to March 2028, excluding men aged 23-60. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31192, @UkraineNow/82187)

Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end according to Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Syrsky, representing a major escalation in drone warfare capacity along the entire front. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193)

Russian forces launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia using tactical aviation, injuring at least six people. FPV drone calculations also destroyed AFU armored vehicles on the Dobropillya direction according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @kpszsu/66527, @tass_agency/382612, @UkraineNow/82190)

A MiG-31K took off from Savastleyka airfield, capable of carrying the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal ballistic missile which is described as not interceptable in most regions. This poses a significant escalation risk for front-line and rear areas. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082)

Multiple drone groups launched from Belgorod Oblast toward Kharkiv Oblast and northern Sumy Oblast, with additional drone activity from Donetsk toward Kharkiv Oblast and from Poltava toward Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66537, @kpszsu/66521)

Russian Ministry of Defense reported shooting down 660 Ukrainian drones overnight. The UN stated it is tracking circumstances of harm to civilians from a Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobilsk. (Source: @UkraineNow/82184, @tass_agency/382606)

Russian Ambassador Trofimov stated Russia aims to systematically nullify the effect of Western unilateral sanctions rather than merely mitigate them. Russia also raised pensions for disabled and 80+ citizens effective July 1. (Source: @tass_agency/382605, @tass_agency/382602)

Drone groups moved from Luhansk Oblast toward Kharkiv Oblast, heading for Chuhuyiv and Kharkiv, while additional strike drones attacked Kharkiv Oblast from Donetsk and Sumy directions. (Source: @kpszsu/66537, @kpszsu/66533)

A strike drone in the Slobozhanske area of Kharkiv Oblast was heading south, with additional drone activity on eastern Kharkiv Oblast toward Kupiansk and eastern Dnipropetrovsk Oblast toward Zaporizhzhia Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66532, @kpszsu/66513)

Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb strikes on the city of Zaporizhzhia, injuring at least six people. A threat of aviation weapon use was declared for multiple front-line oblasts. (Source: @kpszsu/66527, @UkraineNow/82190, @kpszsu/66541)
Drone activity reported near Berestyn and from Donetsk toward Blyzniuky and Lozova in Kharkiv Oblast, indicating continued pressure on the Kharkiv-Zaporizhzhia axis. (Source: @eRadarrua/98339)
Russian FPV drone calculations destroyed Ukrainian armored vehicles on the Dobropillya direction in Donetsk Oblast according to Russian Ministry of Defense reports. (Source: @tass_agency/382612)

Drone groups headed for Chernihiv from the north and for Kharkiv from the north, with additional drone activity near Senkivka on the Chernihiv Oblast border area. (Source: @kpszsu/66519, @eRadarrua/98329)
A drone group from Belgorod Oblast headed toward northern Kharkiv Oblast targeting Bohodukhiv, with additional drones heading for Kharkiv from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66521, @kpszsu/66514)

A reactive-type drone from Sumy Oblast headed toward Poltava Oblast targeting Poltava, while a drone group from Poltava Oblast headed toward Dnipropetrovsk Oblast targeting Mahdalynivka. (Source: @kpszsu/66539, @kpszsu/66537)

Alert declared for threat of strike drone use across Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, with tactical aviation activity noted on northeastern and southeastern directions. (Source: @kpszsu/66530, @kpszsu/66541)

Ukrainian forces struck Russian military vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyakta' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, targeting underwater reconnaissance and mine-laying capabilities. An S-400 radar station covering the Kerch Strait was also hit. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

Four drone groups flew over Black Sea waters from the spit toward Chornomorsk on a previous course, with Ukrainian interceptors deployed to prevent drones from reaching the coast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98337, @eRadarrua/98339)

A drone group headed for Zaporizhzhia from the south, while Russian forces used tactical aviation to launch guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia city, injuring at least six people. (Source: @kpszsu/66521, @UkraineNow/82190)

A reactive drone from Sumy Oblast headed toward Zaporizhzhia Oblast near Malokaterynivka in the Riptsi area, and a drone was reported in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast near Lykhivka. (Source: @eRadarrua/98319, @eRadarrua/98335)
Power outages were being scheduled in Crimea following the strikes on military infrastructure, indicating Ukrainian strikes disrupted occupied territory logistics. (Source: @eRadarrua/98281)

A MiG-31K took off from Savastleyka airfield, serving as a carrier for the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal ballistic missile described as not interceptable in most regions, posing a severe threat to front-line and rear areas. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082)

Russian Ministry of Defense reported intercepting 660 Ukrainian drones overnight, indicating massive scale of Ukrainian drone operations against Russian territory and military targets. (Source: @UkraineNow/82184)

Multiple aerial alerts were declared across Ukrainian oblasts including Kyiv, with air raid sirens activating at 19:09 and lifting at 19:24 in the capital on June 26, 2026. (Source: @eRadarrua/98282, @eRadarrua/98285)

Four drone groups operated over Black Sea waters heading toward Chornomorsk, with Ukrainian interceptors deployed. Drone threats were tracked across Poltava, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts. (Source: @eRadarrua/98337, @kpszsu/66530)

Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia, while FPV drone calculations on both sides struck armored vehicles — Russian drones hit Ukrainian vehicles on Dobropillya direction and Ukrainian drones targeted Russian assets. (Source: @kpszsu/66527, @tass_agency/382612)

key_developments: Russia plans to escalate FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end, representing a fivefold increase in drone warfare intensity across all front-line areas according to Commander-in-Chief Syrsky. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193)

Ukraine struck Russian military vessels 'Volga,' 'Vyakta,' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, eliminating platforms designed for underwater reconnaissance and mine-laying, and also hit an S-400 radar station covering the Kerch Strait. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

A MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier took off from Savastleyka airfield, deploying a weapon system described as not interceptable in most regions, posing an elevated threat to multiple Ukrainian oblasts. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082)

Ukrainian drones struck the Russian 'Azot' chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, one of Russia's largest chemical facilities, also damaging power lines and a residential building with a woman reportedly injured. (Source: @UkraineNow/82184)

A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian soldiers home, all captured in 2022, including defenders of Mariupol, Azovstal, and positions across eight front-line directions. (Source: @UkraineNow/82195)

Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia injuring at least six people, while Russian FPV drones destroyed Ukrainian armored vehicles on the Dobropillya direction in Donetsk Oblast. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190, @tass_agency/382612)

Three Polish politicians declined Ukrainian awards amid deteriorating bilateral relations, while the European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainians to March 2028 excluding men aged 23-60. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31192, @UkraineNow/82187)
sections.threat_assessment: The most significant threat is Russia's planned escalation of FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end, which would fundamentally alter the tactical environment across all front-line oblasts. The scramble of a MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier from Savastleyka introduces a high-speed, difficult-to-intercept strike capability that could target critical infrastructure and military assets deep in Ukrainian territory. Russian tactical aviation's use of guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia and continued FPV drone strikes on armored vehicles in Donetsk Oblast indicate sustained offensive pressure on the eastern and southern fronts. Ukraine's successful strikes on Crimean naval infrastructure and the Novomoskovsk chemical plant demonstrate continued deep-strike capability, but the interception of 660 drones overnight suggests Ukraine must expand air defense coverage. The deterioration of Ukraine-Poland relations and domestic mobilization challenges compound the operational picture.
sections.regional_response: The European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainians to March 2028, though the measure excludes men aged 23-60 and those already granted protection, reflecting a calibrated approach to sustained displacement. European countries placed health services on heightened alert due to a record heat wave, with France confirming 55 heat-related deaths including two children left in a car and Paris reaching 40.9°C. NATO's upcoming July 7-8 summit in Ankara is expected to generate increased Ukrainian public diplomacy activity. Poland's political leadership increasingly distances itself from Ukrainian honors amid historical grievances over the Volyn massacre, signaling potential friction in a key logistics corridor. Ukraine's Budanov characterized the Poland scandal as a 'terrible mistake' but downplayed it as a 'contradiction' rather than a conflict.
sections.intelligence_notes: The 160-person prisoner exchange with all captives from 2022 suggests Russia retains a significant pool of longer-term prisoners for future negotiations, while Zelensky's claim of over 9,500 total returns since the war's start indicates sustained diplomatic back-channels. The reported interception of 660 Ukrainian drones in one night by Russian air defenses represents an extraordinarily high figure that, if accurate, indicates either massive Ukrainian sortie rates or potential Russian inflation of defense claims. The alert regarding a reactive drone heading from Sumy Oblast toward Zaporizhzhia Oblast near Malokaterynivka and Riptsi suggests continued Ukrainian efforts to strike logistics nodes in the southern direction. Russian Ambassador Trofimov's statement about systematically nullifying Western sanctions indicates Moscow's confidence in evasion networks. The timing of Zelensky's public statements about a 'turning point' and '40-day operation to force Russia to peace' ahead of the Ankara NATO summit suggests a coordinated media strategy to secure additional Western aid packages.
